rulings.Case Title: RAMON GOMEZ VS. ZERS DEVELOPMENT, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion By Cross-Defendant City Of South San Francisco For Change Of Venue; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Todd H. Master; Request For Judicial Notice; Proposed Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 20, 2018, Line 1, CROSS DEFENDANT CITY OF SOUTH SAN FRANCISCO's Motion By Cross-Defendant City Of South San Francisco For Change Of Venue. Cross-defendant City of South San Francisco's motion to change venue to San Mateo County is denied and its alternate motion to sever the Shek cross-complaint and transfer that cross-complaint to San Mateo County is also denied. Per still valid and binding case law, a cross-complaint for indemnity and contribution is not an action for "injury" based on "negligence" within the meaning of CCP 394. (Tutor-Saliba-Perini Joint Venture v. Sup. Crt. (1991) 233 Cal.App.3d 736, 743-744; City of Chico v. Sup. Crt. (1979) 89 Cal.App.3d 187, 191.) While this court may disagree with the reasoning underlying this rule of law, this court is required to follow and apply holdings of all published court of appeal decisions where, as here, those holdings have neither been overruled nor disagreed with. The City of South San Francisco fails to show that venue should be transferred per CCP 395. The City does not present evidence defeating all other bases for venue under that statute. The City does not present sufficient evidence showing that venue should be transferred based on the convenience of witnesses and ends of justice. (CCP 397.) The City merely relies on the pleadings and a declaration from counsel, which is insufficient. The request to sever the cross-complaint and transfer that portion of the action to San Mateo County is contrary to the interests of judicial economy. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Gomez is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY W. FOURDYCE ET AL VS. 626 JACKSON STREET LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 20, 2018, Line 1, DEFENDANTS 626 JACKSON STREET LLC, JULIE LEE'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TERRY SCHENK VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Deposition Testimony And To Produce Documents And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 20, 2018, Line 3, PLAINTIFF TERRY SCHENK'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Deposition Testimony And To Produce Documents And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The Court is inclined to order an in-camera review of Mr. Celeste's personnel file to determine relevance and protect his privacy. The Court must balance Mr. Celeste's privacy versus Plaintiff's right to obtain discovery that would assist him at trial. The Court does not believe at this juncture that Plaintiff is entitled to a whole scale production of Mr. Celeste's personnel file, but may be entitled to limited portions if they relate to other complaints or discipline regarding incidents similar to what is alleged in this case. If any documents are produced after the in-camera review, then Plaintiff would be entitled to answers to some of the deposition questions in which he was instructed not to answer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GILBERT PAPAZIAN II VS. KEVIN MICHAEL SULLIVAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Griffins Notice Of Motion For Relief From Default
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 20, 2018, Line 2, DEFENDANT SUSAN GRIFFIN's Motion For Relief From Default. Defendant Susan Griffin's motion for relief from default is granted. The default entered against Ms. Griffin on January 23, 2018 is set aside and Ms. Griffin must file her answer no later than August 31, 2018. Ms. Griffin has shown sufficient grounds to set aside the default based on both the discretionary and mandatory relief provisions of CCP 473(b). Plaintiff Gilbert Papazian is estopped to assert that the portion of Ms. Griffin's motion seeking to set aside the default based on the discretionary relief provisions of CCP 473(b) is untimely due to his counsel's filing of a notice of unavailability for the period July 3, 2018 through July 26, 2018. The court credits the statements of Ms. Griffin's counsel that but for the notice of unavailability and his desire to comply with it this motion would have been filed within 6 months of the entry of default. Where, as here, judgment has not been entered, there is no deadline for the filing of a motion based on the mandatory relief provisions of CCP 473(b), so that part of the motion is timely even absent estoppel. Had Mr. Papazian and his counsel comported themselves in a manner consistent with the conduct expected of all litigants and their counsel, this motion would not have been required to be filed and thus no fees are awarded to Mr. Papazian in opposing the motion. The January 22, 2018 email of Mr. Papazian's counsel providing less than 24 hours' notice of his intent to seek the default of Ms. Griffin while knowing that Ms. Griffin's counsel may either be ill or on vacation falls well below the conduct expected of all counsel in civil litigation. The failure of Mr. Papazian to stipulate to setting aside the default as stated in the January 29, 2018 email of his counsel after learning that Ms. Griffin's counsel did not file an answer due to his illness and the illness of two of his children was yet further uncivil and unwarranted conduct. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Ms. Griffin is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THEODORE WILSON ET AL VS. SCOTT PETERSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Scott Peterson'S Motion To (1) Compel Arbitration And (2) Stay Of Action Until The Completion Of Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 20, 2018, Line 3, Defendant Scott Peterson'S Motion To (1) Compel Arbitration And (2) Stay Of Action Until The Completion Of Arbitration. Defendant Scott Peterson's motion to compel arbitration and to stay action until the completion of arbitration is granted. This case is stayed pending completion of arbitration proceedings on all claims alleged by plaintiffs Theodore Wilson and Tradingpost Modern LLC. The 2014 Operating Agreement includes an arbitration clause with a broad scope that also contains clear and unmistakable language evidencing the parties' intent to delegate issues of arbitrability to the arbitrator. This delegation language is not unconscionable and is enforceable. Plaintiffs have not shown that Mr. Peterson waived his right to enforce the arbitration clause. (St. Agnes Medical Center v. PacifiCare of California (2003) 31 Cal.4th 1187, 1195 ("waivers are not to be lightly inferred and the party seeking to establish a waiver bears a heavy burden of proof.")). Mr. Peterson's actions are not inconsistent with intent to arbitrate. There is no evidence that "the litigation machinery has been substantially invoked." Nor is there any evidence of bad faith delaying tactics by Mr. Peterson. Most significantly plaintiffs have not shown how they have been prejudiced by any litigation conduct by Mr. Peterson. (Groom v. Health Net (2000) 82 Cal.App.4th 1189, 1197 ("Waiver does not occur by mere participation in litigation. Prejudice in the context of waiver of the right to compel arbitration normally means some impairment of the other party's ability to participate in arbitration.")). Bodine v. United Aircraft Corp. (1975) 52 Cal.App.3d 940, 943-944 is distinguishable because there, defendant "[w]ithout ever asserting its right to arbitrate, [] participated in extensive litigation through the pleading, discovery, trial, new trial and appeal stages" culminating in a 10 year delay to demand arbitration. Since Mr. Peterson disputes that he has dissociated from the company because he has not been paid the value of his membership interest, the validity of the Amended Operating Agreement and all related issues such as the arbitrability of parties' disputes whether Mr. Wilson dissociated from Tradingpost Modern and whether Mr. Wilson had the authority to amend the original operating agreement must be decided by the arbitrator. Whether the parties entered into a valid settlement agreement is also to be decided by the arbitrator. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Peterson is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GREGORY P. TORLAI JR. VS. PAMELA RAMIREZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 20, 2018, Line 5, DEFENDANT WILLIAM ROBINSON, WILLIAM G ROBINSON, ACCOUNTANCY CORPORATION, PAMELA RAMIREZ' DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants Pamela Ramirez, William Robinson, and William G. rulings.Case Title: GREGORY P. TORLAI JR. VS. PAMELA RAMIREZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Defendant For And Order Compelling Responses To Interrogatories And The Inspection Demands, That The Genuineness Of Documents And The Truth Of Matters Specified In Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted, And For A Monetary Sanction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 20, 2018, Line 10, DEFENDANTS WILLIAM G ROBINSON, ACCOUNTANCY CORPORATION, PAMELA RAMIREZ'S Motion By Defendant For And Order Compelling Responses To Interrogatories And The Inspection Demands, That The Genuineness Of Documents And The Truth Of Matters Specified In Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted, And For A Monetary Sanction. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. The RFAs will be deemed admitted unless Plaintiff provides responses at or before the hearing on this matter. Supplemental responses to be served by September 14, 2018. Sanctions of $900 imposed against Plaintiff. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: EUGENE BOWEN VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses From Plaintiff To Defendant'S Form Interrogatories And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 20, 2018, Line 11, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion To Compel Further Responses From Plaintiff To Defendant'S Form Interrogatories And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. While responses to Form Interrogatory 17.1 do not require the level of detail articulated in Defendant's papers, the Court finds that there are numerous ambiguities in Plaintiff's responses that require supplementation. For instance, several responses refer collectively to "Defendants" without identifying the specific Defendants whom Plaintiff is making accusations. Also, references to statutes and codes must articulate the specific statute and codes. Supplemental responses to be served by September 14, 2018. Sanctions denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL DISANTO VS. COTCHETT, PITRE & MCCARTHY, LLP. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendants Cotchett Pitre And Mccarthy Llp And Philip Gregory To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 20, 2018, Line 10, DEFENDANT COTCHETT, PITRE & MCCARTHY, LLP., PHILIP GREGORY's Motion Of Defendants Cotchett Pitre And Mccarthy Llp And Philip Gregory To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings. Defendants Philip Gregory and Cotchett, Pitre & McCarthy, LLP's motion to compel arbitration and stay proceedings of all claims in the complaint filed by plaintiff Michael DiSanto is granted. This case is stayed pending conclusion of arbitration proceedings between the parties. Absent a separate written agreement governing CPM's representation of Mr. DiSanto for Bingham's counterclaims against Mr. DiSanto, the arbitration agreement in the Retainer Agreement covers CPM's representation of Mr. DiSanto as to the counterclaims and requires that the parties arbitrate all claims alleged by Mr. DiSanto in this case. The evidence on this motion shows that prior to signing the Retainer Agreement, the parties contemplated the possibility that Bingham might assert counterclaims against Mr. DiSanto relating to Mr. DiSanto's employment agreement with Bingham. While CPM and Mr. DiSanto did not enter into a separate written agreement explicitly providing for CPM to defend Mr. DiSanto against Bingham's counterclaims, CPM represented Mr. DiSanto for the entire action and advanced the costs of arbitration for all claims and counterclaims per the Retainer Agreement. Mr. DiSanto thereafter refused to reimburse CPM for the advanced costs, arguing that per the Retainer Agreement, CPM was not entitled to reimbursement since Mr. DiSanto did not recover anything in the arbitration. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/HEK)
